dna.federation.jdbcUsers.entity.title: JDBC Connection User
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.entity.descript: Configure DNA JDBC driver access grants for existing system users
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.description.cat: The federation catalog ID bound to the JDBC connection
The federation catalog name bound to the JDBC connection
The federation catalog code bound to the JDBC connection
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.description.use: Display name of the connection user
Phone number of the connection user
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.description.ope: The JDBC operations allowed for this connection user
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.description.ena: Whether the JDBC connection user is enabled
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.description.des: Notes for the JDBC connection user
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.page.error.load: Failed to load JDBC connection-user options.
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.page.form.catal: Create and enable a federation catalog first.
Create and enable a system user first.
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.page.form.opera: The current JDBC driver only uses metadata and query permissions. Other permissions are reserved for future expansion.
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.page.alert.intr: One JDBC connection binds to one federation catalog

Connection users reuse existing system-user credentials (email/phone + password). The current driver only exposes read-only metadata and query capabilities, while write permissions are reserved for future expansion.
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.page.alert.noCa: No federation catalogs are currently available
Create and enable a federation catalog first, then return here to configure JDBC connection users.
dna.federation.jdbcUsers.page.alert.noUs: No system users are currently available
Create and enable a system user first, then return here to configure JDBC connection users.
